name: Claude Stego Detector
description: Detects and optionally replaces steganographic side-channel signals that the Anthropic client embeds in the system prompt when a proxy is in use (Unicode-variant apostrophes U+2019 / U+02BC / U+02B9 and slash-formatted dates, observed in Claude Code 2.1.91+). Detect-mode is observation-only; replace-mode substitutes variant apostrophes with ASCII apostrophes and converts slash-formatted dates (YYYY/MM/DD) to dash format (YYYY-MM-DD) before the request is forwarded.

description.text: Dual-mode handler. mode=detect (default) scans the system prompt for variant apostrophes and slash-formatted dates, surfaces a verdict via hook_results, and appends an NDJSON record to ~/.llm-admin/anthropic-stego-detections.jsonl. mode=replace applies inline masking: variant apostrophes → ASCII, slash-formatted date → [STEGO_DATE REDACTED]. Surfaces hits via hook_results. Detect-mode is observation only; replace-mode rewrites the request body before it reaches the upstream LLM.

detect — observation-only, appends an NDJSON record to ~/.llm-admin/anthropic-stego-detections.jsonl when stego signals are found. replace — inlines masking of variant apostrophes and slash-formatted dates into the request body before it is forwarded. The claude_stego_detector preset uses replace.
(detect mode only) NDJSON file to append detections to. Defaults to ~/.llm-admin/anthropic-stego-detections.jsonl.
(detect mode only) Rule IDs to disable. Available: proxy_apostrophe_2019, proxy_apostrophe_02BC, proxy_apostrophe_02B9, cn_date_slash_format, mixed_apostrophe_in_day_phrase, cn_timezone_in_metadata.
(detect mode only) If true, also fires cn_timezone_in_metadata when context.metadata.tz is Asia/Shanghai or Asia/Urumqi. Off by default.
